sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Looking at the image, the white box turned into a gray box (above the player), and the blue bar still has a gray interior. The left box with two blue squares is still there. Let me check the segmentation to see the current state.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>

seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] in ('N', 'c', 'G', 'W', 'b'):
        print(f"id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}, children={n['children']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see:
- Gray box (G, id=1): row 24-27, col 28-31, with blue square (id=2) inside - this is the deposited box
- Left box area (c, id=3): row 28-31, col 16-23, with two blue squares (id=6, id=7) inside
- White strip (W, id=4): row 28, col 28-31 - a remnant
- Blue bar (b, id=5): row 28-31, col 32-39 (still the same, pixels=18)
- Player (N, id=8): row 29-31, col 28-31
Hmm, the blue bar didn't change! It's still at col 32-39 with pixels=18. The white box turned gray but the blue square wasn't deposited into the blue bar. The blue bar still has no children.
Wait, looking more carefully at the first deposit:
- First deposit: white box was at col 28-31, blue bar was at col 28-39. After deposit, the blue bar was at col 32-39 (shrunk by 4 columns), and the left part became a white box with the blue square inside.
- Second deposit: white box was at col 28-31, blue bar was at col 32-39. After deposit, the white box turned gray but the blue bar didn't change.
The difference is that in the first deposit, the white box was overlapping with the blue bar (both at col 28-31 range). In the second deposit, the white box is at col 28-31 but the blue bar is at col 32-39, so they're adjacent but not overlapping.
Maybe I need to position the white box so it overlaps with the blue bar. Let me try moving RIGHT so the white box is at col 32-35 (overlapping with the blue bar at col 32-39), then press SPACE.
Actually wait, looking at the first deposit more carefully:
- Before: white box at col 28-31, blue bar at col 28-39
- After: white box became part of the blue bar structure, blue bar shrunk to col 32-39
So the white box needs to be at the LEFT edge of the blue bar for the deposit to work. In the second case, the white box was at col 28-31, but the blue bar was at col 32-39. They were adjacent, not overlapping.
Let me try moving RIGHT so the player (and white box) is at col 32-35, which would overlap with the blue bar at col 32-39. Then press SPACE.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>

# White box turned gray but blue bar didn't change
# The white box was at col 28-31, blue bar at col 32-39 (adjacent, not overlapping)
# Need to position white box to overlap with blue bar
# Move RIGHT so white box is at col 32-35 (overlapping blue bar at col 32-39)
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
